events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes a missing lockable disconnect for maintenance on a motor circuit that is in-service. Per NEC Article 430, motor disconnects must be capable of being locked in the open (off) position for maintenance. A missing lockable disconnect is a safety/non-compliance issue. This is a fail decision with high or critical risk because workers cannot safely isolate the motor for maintenance.

    "Motor circuit lacks a lockable disconnect, preventing lockout/tagout isolation for maintenance",
    "Non-compliant with NEC Art. 430 disconnect requirements for motor circuits"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize and lock out the motor circuit by an alternative approved means until a proper lockable disconnect is installed",
    "Install a lockable disconnect switch within sight and within required distance of the motor per NEC Art. 430",
    "Verify disconnect rating and short-circuit withstand suitability for the motor load"
  ],
  "rationale": "A missing lockable disconnect means the motor cannot be safely isolated for maintenance, creating an electrocution/energization hazard. NEC Art. 430 requires a disconnecting means for each motor controller and motor, and it must be capable of being locked in the open position. In-service equipment without this safeguard is non-compliant and unsafe.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 430 - Part IX (Disconnecting Means for Motors and Controllers)",
    "NEC Art. 430.102 (Disconnect shall be in sight and lockable)"
  ]
